name: How should I prepare my home before the flooring installers arrive?
headline: How should I prepare my home before the flooring installers arrive?

mainEntity.name: How should I prepare my home before the flooring installers arrive?
mainEntity.text: How should I prepare my home before the flooring installers arrive?

acceptedAnswer.text: To prepare your home for flooring installers, think about access, safety, and protecting your belongings. First, clear the rooms as much as you can. Move small furniture, décor, and anything breakable. Ask in advance whether the installers will move large items like sofas, beds, and refrigerators, and whether there is an extra fee. Empty closets if the floors inside will be replaced. Plan for appliances. Refrigerators, stoves, washers, and dryers may need to be disconnected and moved. Confirm who is responsible for disconnecting water, gas, and power. If you are not comfortable doing that, you may need a plumber or appliance tech. Next, figure out where the crew can park and set up tools. Let them know about steep driveways or tight streets, which are common in Pacifica and Daly City. Keep pets and kids away from the work area for safety. You can also tape plastic over doorways or turn off your HVAC to help reduce dust spreading to other rooms. If you are tearing out the old floors yourself, make a plan to haul away the debris. Keep an eye on nails, staples, and sharp edges. Lastly, discuss start times, bathroom access, and where they can plug in equipment so there are no surprises on the installation day.
